role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; co-subject of a joint oral history with her husband John Rosenberg (catalog entry 61).
ai_reading: Jean Rosenberg shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 61 (catalog slug 'john-and-jean-rosenberg'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Jean Rosenberg is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 61 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Jean Rosenberg; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for Jean Rosenberg is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 61
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Former Floyd County Courthouse, Prestonsburg, Kentucky
gallery.caption: The former Floyd County Courthouse in Prestonsburg, Kentucky, the county seat where Jean Rosenberg settled with her husband John, who founded the Appalachian Research and Defense Fund there in 1970.
